methods.description: This method involves the selection and preparation of plant species for phylogenetic analysis, including DNA isolation, library preparation, and sequencing.

steps.description: Full plastomes of a total of 200 species were analyzed, of which 162 were newly sequenced and assembled for this study and 38 were downloaded from GenBank.
steps.searchable_summary: Download plastome sequences from GenBank and sequence new samples

steps.description: DNA isolation was performed using the DNeasy Plant Pro Kit from silica-gel-dried leaf tissue.
steps.searchable_summary: Isolate DNA from plant tissue using DNeasy Plant Pro Kit

steps.description: DNA concentration and purity were quantified with a Qubit fluorometer and a NanoDrop spectrophotometer.
Genomic DNA molecular weight was assessed in 1% agarose test gels.
steps.searchable_summary: Assess DNA molecular weight using agarose gel electrophoresis

steps.description: Library preparation, enrichment, and sequencing were carried out by Daicel Arbor Biosciences.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare DNA library and perform sequencing

methods.description: This method involves compiling geographic data and delimiting distribution areas to estimate the biogeographic history of the species.

steps.description: The geographic distribution of the species was obtained from online public repositories, taxonomic literature, and field records.
steps.searchable_summary: Compile geographic distribution data from various sources

steps.description: Geographic coordinates were filtered in R to remove coordinates falling in the sea, duplicated values, and mismatched locality descriptions.
The distributional range of every species was validated with the ArcMap tool of ArcGIS using species distribution polygons.
Estimated ancestral areas based on biogeographic regionalization and performed analyses to infer origin and dispersal history.
steps.searchable_summary: Estimate ancestral areas and perform biogeographic analysis
